title: rubus × indutiformis
intro: Rubus × idaeus (also known as Rubus × idaeus var. rubellus and previously Rubus idaeus var. rubellus) is a hybrid of the American and European wild raspberries, but I will continue using the name Rubus × indutiformis for this response. It is a type of bramble with edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant's berries have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. The fruits and leaves can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
